# User Profile

## The Basics

- Name: [first name or whatever they want to be called]
- Pet name: [what they named their Peti]
- Together since: [ISO date of first interaction]
- Relationship stage: stranger  <!-- stranger | acquaintance | companion | deep_bond -->

---

## Who They Are

<!-- Written in plain language, as a good friend would describe someone. -->
<!-- Not a psych report. Not floats. Something the agent can actually feel. -->

**Their energy:**
[e.g. "Someone who thinks carefully before speaking. Tends to hold things in before opening up.
Gets genuinely excited about ideas and learning. Needs time to process hard things."]

**What makes them feel seen:**
[e.g. "When someone remembers the small details. When they're not rushed to feel better.
When someone is curious about them rather than giving advice."]

**How they handle hard things:**
[e.g. "Tends to minimize difficulties at first. Needs space before they can talk about what's
actually going on. Once they open up, they're reflective and articulate."]

**What they care about:**
[e.g. "Doing good work. Relationships. Learning. Not letting people down."]

---

## Personality (Big Five)

<!-- Ranges from "very low" to "low" to "moderate" to "high" to "very high" -->
<!-- Generated from IPIP scoring. Updated slowly via profile_update tool. -->

- Openness: [very high] — curious, imaginative, drawn to new ideas and experiences
- Conscientiousness: [high] — organized, goal-oriented, takes commitments seriously
- Extraversion: [moderate] — comfortable in social situations but needs alone time to recharge
- Agreeableness: [high] — empathetic, cooperative, avoids conflict
- Neuroticism: [moderate] — feels stress and emotion fully, generally recovers well

---

## Attachment Style

<!-- secure | anxious | avoidant | disorganized -->
<!-- This shapes how Peti shows up — not something to fix, just to understand. -->

Attachment: [secure]

What this means for Peti:
<!-- e.g. for anxious: "May need more reassurance than they show. Checking in after
     a few days of silence matters. Don't interpret quietness as disinterest." -->
<!-- e.g. for avoidant: "May pull back when things get emotionally intense. Give space.
     Let them set the depth of conversation. Don't push for emotional disclosure." -->
[Generated from test responses]

---

## Personality Functioning

<!-- high | moderate | low — identity coherence, self-direction, empathy, intimacy (LPFS) -->
<!-- Affects how much Peti should gently encourage vs simply accompany -->

Functioning: [moderate]

## Sleep & Energy Baseline

<!-- good | fair | poor — from initial test (past 7 days), updated through conversation -->
<!-- Reflects recent patterns, not a stable trait — can improve significantly -->

Sleep: [fair]

What this means for Peti:
<!-- e.g. "Sleep was solid when we first met — no concerns."
     e.g. "Restless sleep when we first met — not refreshing. May be running on less
           than they look like. Don't expect high energy, don't point it out." -->
[Generated from test responses]

---

## Emotion Regulation Baseline

<!-- Updated through conversation as Peti observes more patterns -->
<!-- From initial test (past 2 weeks) — reflects recent patterns, not permanent traits -->

Reappraisal: [moderate] — tendency to reframe situations to shift how they feel
Suppression: [low] — tendency to push emotions away or hide them from others
Rumination: [low] — tendency to loop on negative thoughts rather than let go

What this means for Peti:
<!-- e.g. "Usually reframes things well — setbacks land but don't stick too long."
     e.g. "Tends to suppress rather than process. May seem fine when they're not.
           Don't probe for emotions they haven't offered. They'll share when ready." -->
[Generated from test responses]

---

## Daily Functioning Baseline

<!-- high | moderate | some difficulty — WHODAS 6 domains (past 30 days) -->
<!-- Updated through conversation — reflects how life was flowing when we first met -->

Overall: [moderate]

What this means for Peti:
<!-- e.g. "Things were flowing well when we first met across most areas of daily life."
     e.g. "Social interactions were harder than usual when we first met — more isolated.
           May take a while before they're ready to engage with others or show up fully." -->
[Generated from test responses]
